title: microcos paucicostata
intro: Microcos paucicostata, also known as the 'Five-ribbed Microcos', is a plant species belonging to the family Malvaceae and is native to Southeast Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which contribute to its health benefits such as wound healing, reducing fever, and alleviating digestive issues. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, to support overall well-being. Furthermore, its leaves and roots are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including diarrhea, dysentery, and respiratory problems.
